queries.query_text: Add RequestBodySizeLimit property to DefaultHttpContext surfacing IHttpMaxRequestBodySizeFeature and throw BadHttpRequestException status 413 in BufferingHelper when limit exceeded

solve_notes: Added `RequestBodySizeLimit` property to `DefaultHttpContext` that reads/writes `IHttpMaxRequestBodySizeFeature.MaxRequestBodySize` via the feature cache. Also added `SizeLimitedBufferingReadStream` that wraps `FileBufferingReadStream` and throws `BadHttpRequestException` (status 413) instead of plain `IOException` when the limit is exceeded. Modified `BufferingHelper.EnableRewind` to automatically use the context limit when no explicit `bufferLimit` is passed.
task_text: `DefaultHttpContext` in `src/Http/Http/src/DefaultHttpContext.cs` does not enforce request body size limits by default. When `MaxRequestBodySize` is configured on the server, the limit is checked at the server layer but not surfaced in `HttpContext` for middleware inspection. Add a `RequestBodySizeLimit` property to `DefaultHttpContext` that exposes the configured limit and throws `BadHttpRequestException` when the body exceeds it during buffered reads.

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers what a developer must see to implement the property and the feature integration. The thrash-preventing set adds SizeLimitedBufferingReadStream (the enforcement mechanism) and BadHttpRequestException (to know its constructor). An AI agent would search for BadHttpRequestException constructors upfront and would need to know about SizeLimitedBufferingReadStream's existence to understand how enforcement works without re-reading the entire stream pipeline.
